URL: https://byjus.com/biology/biofertilizers/

SUMMARY:
Biofertilizers are substance that contains microbes, which helps in promoting the growth of plants and trees by increasing the supply of essential nutrients to the plants . It comprises living organisms which include mycorrhizal fungi, blue-green algae, and bacteria.

FULL CONTENT:
![](https://www.facebook.com/tr?id=506170199519639&ev=PageView&noscript=1)

## Biofertilizers Definition

***“Biofertilizers are substances that contain microorganisms, which when added to the soil increase its fertility and promotes plant growth.”***

![Biofertilizers](https://cdn1.byjus.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/biology/2017/05/27073155/Biofertilizers.png "Biofertilizers")

![Biofertilizers](https://cdn1.byjus.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/biology/2017/05/27073155/Biofertilizers.png "Biofertilizers")

## What is Biofertilizer?

Biofertilizers are substance that contains microbes,  which helps in promoting the growth of plants and trees by increasing the supply of essential nutrients to the plants. It comprises living organisms which include mycorrhizal fungi, blue-green algae, and bacteria. Mycorrhizal fungi preferentially withdraw minerals from organic matter for the plant whereas cyanobacteria are characterized by the property of nitrogen fixation.

Nitrogen fixation is defined as a process of converting di-nitrogen molecules into ammonia. For instance, some bacteria convert nitrogen to ammonia. As a result, nitrogen becomes available for plants.

Also, read [Nitrogen Fixation and Nitrogen Metabolism](https://byjus.com/biology/nitrogen-fixation-nitrogen-metabolism/)

## Types of Biofertilizers

Following are the important types of biofertilizers:

### Symbiotic Nitrogen-Fixing Bacteria

[Rhizobium](https://byjus.com/biology/rhizobium/) is one of the vital symbiotic nitrogen-fixing bacteria. Here bacteria seek shelter and obtain food from plants. In return, they help by providing fixed nitrogen to the plants.

### Loose Association of Nitrogen-Fixing Bacteria

Azospirillum is a nitrogen-fixing bacteria that live around the roots of higher plants but do not develop an intimate relationship with plants. It is often termed as rhizosphere association as these bacteria collect plant exudate and the same is used as food by them. This process is termed associative mutualism.

### Symbiotic Nitrogen-Fixing Cyanobacteria

Blue-Green algae or Cyanobacteria from the symbiotic association with several plants. Liverworts, cycad roots, fern, and lichens are some of the Nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria. Anabaena is found at the leaf cavities of the fern. It is responsible for nitrogen fixation. The fern plants decay and release the same for utilization of the rice plants. Azolla pinnate is a fern that resides in rice fields but they do not regulate the growth of the plant.

### Free-Living Nitrogen-Fixing Bacteria

They are free-living soil [bacteria](https://byjus.com/biology/bacteria/) that perform nitrogen fixation. They are saprotrophic anaerobes such as *Clostridium beijerinckii*, Azotobacter, etc.

Among all the types of biofertilizers, Rhizobium and Azospirillum are most widely used.

## Components of Biofertilizers

The components of biofertilizers include:

### Bio Compost

It is one of the eco-friendly product composed of waste material released from sugar industries which are decomposed. It is magnified with human-friendly bacteria, fungi, and various plants.

### Tricho-Card

It is an eco-friendly and nonpathogenic product used in a variety of crops as well as in horticultural and ornamental plants, such as paddy apple, sugar cane, brinjal, corn, cotton, vegetables, citrus, etc. It acts as a productive destroyer and antagonistic hyper parasitic against eggs of several bores, shoot, fruit, leaves, flower eaters and other pathogens in the field.

### Azotobacter

It protects the roots from [pathogens](https://byjus.com/biology/pathogen/) present in the soil and plays a crucial role in fixing atmospheric nitrogen. Nitrogen is a very important nutrient for the plant and about 78% of the total atmosphere comprises nitrogen.

### Phosphorus

Phosphorus is one of the essential nutrients for plants growth and development. Phosphate solubilizing microorganisms, hydrolyze insoluble phosphorus compounds to the soluble form for uptake by plants. Many fungi and bacteria are used for the purpose such as *Penicillium,* *Aspergillus, Bacillus, Pseudomonas, etc.*

### Vermicompost

It is an Eco-friendly organic fertilizer that comprises vitamins, hormones, organic carbon, sulfur, antibiotics that help to increase the quantity and quality of yield. Vermicompost is one of the quick fixes to improve the fertility of the soil.

**Also refer:** [Vermicomposting](https://byjus.com/biology/vermicomposting/)

## Applications of Biofertilizers

Following are the important applications of biofertilizers:

### Seedling root dip

This method is applicable to rice crops. The seedlings are planted in the bed of water for 8-10 hours.

### Seed Treatment

The seeds are dipped in a mixture of nitrogen and phosphorus fertilizers. These seeds are then dried and sown as soon as possible.

### Soil Treatment

The biofertilizers along with the compost fertilizers are mixed and kept for one night. This mixture is then spread on the soil where the seeds have to be sown.

Discover more about what is biofertilizer, types of biofertilizers and applications of biofertilizers, only at [BYJU’S Biology](https://byjus.com/biology/)

## Frequently Asked Questions on Biofertilizers

### What do you understand by biofertilizers?

Biofertilizers are substances that contain microorganisms, which when added to the soil increase the crop yield and promotes plant growth.

### What are the advantages of biofertilizers over chemical fertilizers?

### What is the need for using biofertilizers?

Biofertilizers are required to restore the fertility of the soil. Prolonged use of chemical fertilizers degrades the soil and affects crop yield. Biofertilizers, on the other hand, enhance the water holding capacity of the soil and add essential nutrients such as nitrogen, vitamins and proteins to the soil. They are the natural form of fertilizers and hence, widely used in agriculture.

![ROI Biologicals logo](https://roibiologicals.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/logo.svg)

# What Is the Purpose of Biofertilizers? Advantages & Applications Explained

![Copy Link](https://roibiologicals.com//wp-content/themes/wl/assets/imgs/share/link.svg)
![LinkedIn](https://roibiologicals.com//wp-content/themes/wl/assets/imgs/share/linkedin.svg)
![Twitter](https://roibiologicals.com//wp-content/themes/wl/assets/imgs/share/twitter.svg)
![Facebook](https://roibiologicals.com//wp-content/themes/wl/assets/imgs/share/facebook.svg)
![What Is the Purpose of Biofertilizers | ROI Biologicals](https://roibiologicals.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/What-Is-the-Purpose-of-Biofertilizers.jpg)
![What Is the Purpose of Biofertilizers | ROI Biologicals](https://roibiologicals.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/What-Is-the-Purpose-of-Biofertilizers.jpg)

Biofertilizers have gained significant attention in recent years due to their potential to enhance plant growth while promoting sustainable agriculture. Unlike chemical fertilizers, biofertilizers are derived from natural sources and contain living microorganisms that facilitate nutrient uptake and improve soil health. These innovations have allowed farmers to explore the purpose, composition, and advantages of biofertilizers over chemical fertilizers. It is also important to understand the proper applications of biofertilizers. At ROI Biologicals, we not only sell [high-quality biofertilizers](https://roibiologicals.com/products#biofertilizers) but are dedicated to helping farmers of all kinds move toward this more sustainable option.

## ****What Are Biofertilizers?****

Biofertilizers, as substances containing living microorganisms, play a crucial role in promoting plant growth and enhancing soil fertility. These biological fertilizers utilize beneficial microorganisms to increase the supply or availability of primary nutrients to plants. There are three main types of biofertilizers: bacterial biofertilizers, fungal biofertilizers, and algal biofertilizers.

Bacterial biofertilizers, such as Rhizobium, Azospirillum, and Azotobacter, consist of bacteria that form symbiotic relationships with plants. They fix atmospheric nitrogen, providing a natural and sustainable source of this essential nutrient to the host plants. By utilizing bacterial biofertilizers, farmers can reduce their dependence on nitrogen-based chemical fertilizers and enhance the overall nitrogen cycle in the soil.

Fungal biofertilizers, like mycorrhizae and phosphate-solubilizing bacteria, contain fungi that aid in solubilizing phosphorus in the soil. These beneficial fungi establish symbiotic associations with plant roots, extending their reach and enhancing nutrient uptake, particularly phosphorus. By increasing the availability of phosphorus, fungal biofertilizers contribute to improved plant growth, especially in phosphorus-deficient soils.

Algal biofertilizers encompass algae, such as blue-green algae (BGA) and Azolla, which play a dual role in fixing atmospheric nitrogen and producing growth-promoting substances. Blue-green algae are capable of fixing nitrogen through a process called nitrogen fixation, converting atmospheric nitrogen into a form that plants can utilize. Azolla, a water fern that forms a symbiotic relationship with the bacterium Anabaena, also fixes atmospheric nitrogen and can be cultivated in ponds or tanks before being applied to the soil.

As the demand for sustainable agricultural practices grows, biofertilizers have become a promising alternative to chemical fertilizers. Farmers are increasingly adopting biofertilizers to promote eco-friendly farming methods and reduce their ecological footprint. The examples of biofertilizers mentioned earlier illustrate the diversity of microorganisms and plants that can be harnessed to enhance crop growth and soil health.

## ****Why Biofertilizers Matter****

Chemical fertilizers have long been used in agriculture for their ability to provide essential nutrients quickly. However, their excessive and indiscriminate use has led to several challenges. Chemical fertilizers can degrade soil quality, disrupt microbial populations, contribute to water pollution, and cause long-term ecological imbalances. Moreover, the high cost and energy-intensive manufacturing processes associated with chemical fertilizers make them unsustainable in the long run. Biofertilizers offer a viable alternative that addresses these challenges by promoting sustainable agriculture practices.

## ****Advantages of Using Biofertilizers****

### ****Improved Soil Health****

Biofertilizers enhance soil structure, nutrient cycling, and microbial diversity, leading to improved soil health and fertility. They foster a symbiotic relationship with plants, facilitating nutrient absorption and increasing plant resistance to diseases and pests.

### ****Sustainable Agriculture****

Biofertilizers reduce dependence on synthetic chemicals and minimize environmental pollution. They contribute to sustainable agricultural practices by promoting natural nutrient cycling and reducing the negative impact on ecosystems.

### ****Cost-Effectiveness****

Biofertilizers offer long-term cost benefits by reducing the need for chemical fertilizers and other synthetic inputs. They improve soil fertility, leading to higher crop yields and decreased input costs.

### ****Enhanced Nutrient Availability****

The microorganisms in biofertilizers help fix atmospheric nitrogen, solubilize phosphorus, and enhance the availability of other essential nutrients to plants. This leads to improved nutrient uptake and overall plant growth.

### ****Reduced Environmental Impact****

Unlike chemical fertilizers that contribute to water pollution and greenhouse gas emissions, biofertilizers have minimal environmental impact. They support sustainable farming practices by promoting organic and eco-friendly agriculture.

## ****Applications of Biofertilizers****

Biofertilizers can be effectively applied in various agricultural practices, with varying benefits depending on the types of crops being grown and the goals of each farmer.

The primary application is for the improvement of crop production. Biofertilizers are suitable for a wide range of crops, including cereals, legumes, vegetables, and fruits. They can be used in both organic and conventional farming systems to improve crop productivity and quality. Additionally, biofertilizers play a crucial role in the production of high-quality fruits, flowers, and ornamental plants. They promote root development, enhance nutrient uptake, and improve overall plant health.

On a global level, biofertilizers aid in reforestation efforts and land reclamation projects by improving soil fertility and supporting the establishment of plant cover in degraded areas. Biofertilizers are essential components of sustainable farming practices, such as integrated nutrient management, organic farming, and precision agriculture. They contribute to long-term soil health and help maintain ecological balance, making them a great choice for any farmers concerned about the Earth.

## ****Biofertilizers from ROI Biologicals****

At ROI Biologicals, we take pride in being a leading provider of eco-friendly biofertilizers and soil health solutions. Our product range is carefully designed to enhance soil health, increase crop yields, and reduce the dependency on synthetic chemicals.

All of our solutions are eco-friendly and derived from natural sources. Our biofertilizers are formulated with the environment in mind, ensuring minimal environmental impact and reducing reliance on chemical inputs. By embracing sustainable materials, we promote responsible agricultural practices that prioritize the health of our ecosystems.

Furthermore, we understand the importance of providing expert consultation and support to farmers. We offer [free consultations](https://roibiologicals.com/free-consultation/), providing farmers with guidance on improving soil health, enhancing productivity, and achieving higher crop yields. We believe in empowering farmers with the knowledge and tools they need to make informed decisions about soil management and the application of our biofertilizers.

At RIO Biologicals, we pride ourselves on providing customized solutions tailored to the specific needs of farmers. We consider factors such as soil type, crop type, and geographical location when developing biofertilizer recommendations. By offering personalized solutions, we ensure that our biofertilizers are optimized for maximum effectiveness in each unique agricultural context.

[Our biofertilizers](#biofertilizers) have a proven track record of delivering successful results. Through scientific research, rigorous field trials, and positive customer testimonials, we have established a reputation for providing effective and reliable biofertilizers. Our products consistently lead to increased crop yields, improved plant health, and the promotion of sustainable agricultural practices.

Our commitment to environmental sustainability, expert consultation, customized solutions, and proven results make us the best choice for farmers seeking to improve soil health, boost crop yields, and embrace sustainable farming practices. With our comprehensive range of effective biofertilizers and unwavering support, we are here to help farmers achieve their agricultural goals and contribute to a more sustainable future in farming. [Contact us](https://roibiologicals.com/contact/) today to learn more about the greener path toward increasing crop yield.

SUMMARY:
Biofertilizers add nutrients through the natural processes of nitrogen fixation, solubilizing phosphorus, and stimulating plant growth through the synthesis of

FULL CONTENT:
![](/static/images/icons/wikipedia.png)
![Wikipedia](/static/images/mobile/copyright/wikipedia-wordmark-en.svg)
![The Free Encyclopedia](/static/images/mobile/copyright/wikipedia-tagline-en.svg)

## Contents

# Biofertilizer

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article **needs additional or more specific [categories](/wiki/Wikipedia:Categorization "Wikipedia:Categorization")**. Please [help out](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Biofertilizer&action=edit) by [adding categories](/wiki/Wikipedia:FAQ/Categorization#How_do_I_add_an_article_to_a_category? "Wikipedia:FAQ/Categorization") to it so that it can be listed with similar articles. *(March 2025)* |

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/6/6c/Wiki_letter_w.svg/40px-Wiki_letter_w.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/18/Blue-green_algae_cultured_in_specific_media.jpg/250px-Blue-green_algae_cultured_in_specific_media.jpg)

A **biofertilizer** is a substance containing living [micro-organisms](/wiki/Micro-organism "Micro-organism") which, when applied to seeds, plant surfaces, or soil, colonize the [rhizosphere](/wiki/Rhizosphere "Rhizosphere") or the interior of the [plant](/wiki/Plant "Plant") and promotes growth by increasing the supply or availability of primary [nutrients](/wiki/Nutrient "Nutrient") to the host plant.[[1]](#cite_note-vessey2003-1) Biofertilizers add nutrients through the natural processes of [nitrogen fixation](/wiki/Nitrogen_fixation "Nitrogen fixation"), solubilizing [phosphorus](/wiki/Phosphorus "Phosphorus"), and stimulating plant growth through the synthesis of growth-promoting substances. The micro-organisms in biofertilizers restore the soil's natural [nutrient cycle](/wiki/Nutrient_cycle "Nutrient cycle") and build [soil organic matter](/wiki/Soil_organic_matter "Soil organic matter"). Through the use of biofertilizers, healthy plants can be grown, while enhancing the sustainability and the health of the soil. Biofertilizers can be expected to reduce the use of synthetic [fertilizers](/wiki/Fertilizer "Fertilizer") and [pesticides](/wiki/Pesticide "Pesticide"), but they are not yet able to replace their use. As of 2024, more than 340 biofertilizer products have been approved for commercial use in the US.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Composition

Biofertilizers provide "[eco-friendly](/wiki/Environmentally_friendly "Environmentally friendly")" organic agro-inputs. *[Rhizobium](/wiki/Rhizobium "Rhizobium")*, *[Azotobacter](/wiki/Azotobacter "Azotobacter")*, *[Azospirillum](/wiki/Azospirillum_brasilense "Azospirillum brasilense")* and [blue-green algae](/wiki/Blue-green_algae "Blue-green algae") (BGA) are perhaps the species with the longest history of use as biofertilizers. *[Rhizobium](/wiki/Rhizobium "Rhizobium")* inoculant is used for leguminous crops. *[Azotobacter](/wiki/Azotobacter "Azotobacter")* can be used with crops like [wheat](/wiki/Wheat "Wheat"), [maize](/wiki/Maize "Maize"), [mustard](/wiki/Mustard_plant "Mustard plant"), [cotton](/wiki/Cotton "Cotton"), [potato](/wiki/Potato "Potato"), and other vegetable crops. *Azospirillum* inoculations are recommended mainly for [sorghum](/wiki/Sorghum "Sorghum"), [millets](/wiki/Millets "Millets"), [maize](/wiki/Maize "Maize"), [sugarcane](/wiki/Sugarcane "Sugarcane"), and [wheat](/wiki/Wheat "Wheat"). [Blue-green algae](/wiki/Blue-green_algae "Blue-green algae") belonging to the [cyanobacteria](/wiki/Cyanobacteria "Cyanobacteria") [genera](/wiki/Genera "Genera") *[Nostoc](/wiki/Nostoc "Nostoc")*, *[Anabaena](/wiki/Anabaena "Anabaena")*, *Tolypothrix*, and *[Aulosira](/wiki/Aulosira "Aulosira")* [fix atmospheric nitrogen](/wiki/Nitrogen_fixation "Nitrogen fixation") and are used as inoculants for [paddy crops](/wiki/Paddy_field "Paddy field") grown in both upland and lowland conditions. *[Anabaena](/wiki/Anabaena "Anabaena")*, in association with the water fern *[Azolla](/wiki/Azolla "Azolla")*, can contribute nitrogen up to 60 kg/ha/season and can also enrich soils with organic matter.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Seaweeds](/wiki/Seaweed_fertilizer "Seaweed fertilizer") are rich in various types of mineral elements (potassium, phosphorus, trace elements, etc.), hence they are extensively used as a form of manure replacement by people of coastal districts.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Seaweed-fertilizer also helps in breaking down clays.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] *[Fucus](/wiki/Fucus "Fucus")* is used by Irish people as a biofertilizer on a large scale.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] In tropical countries, the bottom mud from dried-up ponds which contain abundant blue-green algae is regularly used as biofertilizer in fields.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7e/The_root-like_Mycelium_of_a_fungus.jpg/250px-The_root-like_Mycelium_of_a_fungus.jpg)

### Bacteria

## Mechanisms

Biofertilizers work through multiple mechanisms. [Plant-growth promoting rhizobacteria](/wiki/Plant-growth_promoting_rhizobacteria "Plant-growth promoting rhizobacteria") (PGPR) and [mycorrhizae](/wiki/Mycorrhizae "Mycorrhizae") are generally thought to increase the [fixation of atmospheric nitrogen](/wiki/Nitrogen_fixation "Nitrogen fixation"),[[17]](#cite_note-17) convert inorganic [phosphorus](/wiki/Phosphorus "Phosphorus") compounds into soluble forms, increase the [bioavailability](/wiki/Bioavailability "Bioavailability") of minerals in the soil,[[18]](#cite_note-18) and synthesize [phytohormones](/wiki/Plant_hormone "Plant hormone") that promote growth, such as [auxins](/wiki/Auxin "Auxin") and [gibberellin](/wiki/Gibberellin "Gibberellin").[[7]](#cite_note-:1-7)[[11]](#cite_note-:3-11) Another mechanism proposed is the [AAC](/wiki/1-Aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylic_acid "1-Aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylic acid")-[deaminase](/wiki/Deamination "Deamination") production of *[Bacillus](/wiki/Bacillus "Bacillus")* species, which prevents excessive increases in the synthesis of [ethylene](/wiki/Ethylene "Ethylene") under various stress conditions.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Benefits

Biofertilizers are cost-effective and ecofriendly in nature, and their continuous usage has been shown to enhance soil fertility.[[20]](#cite_note-:2-20) Besides promoting growth by multiple [mechanisms](#Mechanisms), biofertilizers produces substances suppressing [phytopathogens](/wiki/Plant_disease "Plant disease"), guarding plants from abiotic and biotic stresses and detoxification of belowground [pollutants](/wiki/Toxic_heavy_metal#Environment "Toxic heavy metal").[[21]](#cite_note-21) Extensive use of [agrochemicals](/wiki/Agrochemical "Agrochemical") in agricultural practices has been found to cause environmental disturbances and public health hazards affecting food security and sustainability in agriculture.[[22]](#cite_note-22) Biofertilizers offers an alternative solution for such agrochemicals, and show yield increase of up to about 10–40% by increasing protein contents, essential amino acids, and vitamins, and by nitrogen fixation.[[20]](#cite_note-:2-20)

Since a bio-fertilizer is technically living, it can [symbiotically](/wiki/Symbiotic "Symbiotic") associate with plant roots. Involved microorganisms could readily and safely convert complex organic material into simple compounds, so that they are easily taken up by the plants. Microorganism function is in long duration, causing improvement of the soil fertility. It maintains the natural habitat of the soil. It increases crop yield by 20-30%, replaces chemical [nitrogen](/wiki/Nitrogen "Nitrogen") and [phosphorus](/wiki/Phosphorus "Phosphorus") by 30%, and stimulates plant growth. It can also provide protection against drought and some soil-borne diseases. It has also been shown that to produce a larger quantity of crops, biofertilizers with the ability of nitrogen fixation and phosphorus solubilizing would lead to the greatest possible effect.[[23]](#cite_note-:0-23) They advance shoot and root growth of many crops versus control groups.[[24]](#cite_note-24) This can be important when implementing new seed growth.

## Future Research

Biofertilizers have been shown to have varying effects in different environments,[[25]](#cite_note-25) and even within the same environment. This is something that many scientists have been working on, however there is no perfect solution at this time. They however, have been shown to have the most profound effects in drier climates.[[23]](#cite_note-:0-23) In the future, it is hoped that biofertilizers effects will be better controlled and regulated in all environments, as well as analysis targeted at specific species.

--- SOURCE 5: Biofertilizer - an overview | ScienceDirect Topics ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/agricultural-and-biological-sciences/biofertilizer

SUMMARY:
Biofertilizers are defined as bacterial or fungal inoculants applied to plants to enhance nutrient availability and utilization, or as microbial biostimulants



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/biofertilizers-200425092445/85/bio-fertilizers-definition-types-advantages-and-disadvantages-11-638.jpg?cb=1667924303
DESCRIPTION: The image lists the advantages of bio-fertilizers, highlighting their role as renewable nutrient sources, soil health sustainers, chemical fertilizer substitutes, and their positive effects on crop yield, soil structure, and plant growth without adverse effects.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://images.tractorgyan.com/uploads/105750/64be42a1f28c9_uses-of-biofertilizers.webp
DESCRIPTION: Various types of biofertilizers from plant waste are used for different purposes such as seeding root tip, seed treatment, soil treatment, crop residual, green manure, treated sewage sludge, and farmyard manure.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://assets.tractorjunction.com/tractor-junction/blog/2024/02/What-are-Biofertilizers-6.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the advantages of bio-fertilizers, including improving soil health, preventing plant infection from spreading, and increasing crop yield, with icons representing soil, plants, and crops.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://www.slideteam.net/wp/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Benefits-of-using-biofertilizers-for-plant-growth-and-health.png
DESCRIPTION: An infographic with five sections detailing the benefits of bio-fertilizers, each featuring icons, headings, and brief explanations, all decorated with green leaves and a pastel green textured background.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://image.slideserve.com/316635/advantages-of-biofertilizer-use-n.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The content outlines the advantages of biofertilizers in agriculture, highlighting their ability to provide essential nutrients, enhance soil health, and promote better water absorption while maintaining biological activity in the soil.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Bio-fertilizers are natural substances containing living microorganisms that enhance soil fertility and promote plant growth by increasing nutrient availability.
Bio-fertilizers play a crucial role in sustainable agriculture, improving soil health, reducing chemical dependency, and promoting environmental balance.
Unlike chemical fertilizers, bio-fertilizers are eco-friendly, enhance soil microbial diversity, and improve long-term soil fertility without the adverse effects of synthetic chemicals.
